steps.title: Read the zeros of g
steps.content: The graph of $y=g(x)$ intersects or touches the $x$-axis at $x=-2$, $x=1$, and $x=3$. So $$g(-2)=0,\quad g(1)=0,\quad g(3)=0.$$

steps.title: Translate the zeros through x plus 28
steps.content: The equation is $$g(x+28)=\frac{1}{10}(x-a)^2(x-b)^2(x-c).$$ A zero on the right happens when $x+28$ equals a zero of $g$. So the possible zeros of $g(x+28)$ are $$-2-28=-30,$$ $$1-28=-27,$$ and $$3-28=-25.$$

steps.title: Match the possible value of b
steps.content: The factored form has zeros at $x=a$, $x=b$, and $x=c$, with $a$ and $b$ repeated because they are squared. The question only asks which listed value could be $b$. The possible shifted zero values are $-30$, $-27$, and $-25$; of the answer choices, only $-25$ appears.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice A, $31$, would correspond to a zero of $g$ at $31+28=59$. Choice B, $3$, would correspond to $31$. Choice C, $-2$, would correspond to $26$. None of those are visible zeros of $g$. D, $-25$, corresponds to the visible zero $3$ because $-25+28=3$.
